mnemonics: StringBuffer is for Building & Buffer-ing. Use it when you need to Append, Append, Append!
keyPoints: String objects are immutable; any modification creates a new object.
StringBuffer is mutable and thread-safe, making it efficient for multiple operations.
StringBuilder is also mutable but not thread-safe, offering higher performance in single-threaded contexts.
commonMistakes: Using the '+' operator for concatenation in loops, which creates many temporary String objects and is inefficient.
Using StringBuilder in a multi-threaded environment where StringBuffer should be used for safety.
